expectations.text: Output identifies YEAR(o_orderdate) as non-sargable and explains why it prevents index usage
expectations.evidence: Section 'Problem Analysis': 'The function YEAR(o_orderdate) wraps the indexed column o_orderdate, which prevents MySQL from using idx_orders_o_orderdate... makes the predicate non-sargable (Search ARGument ABLE).'

expectations.text: Rewritten SQL uses a range condition (>= and <) instead of the YEAR() function

notes: Both configurations scored 100% pass rate — the model correctly identifies and fixes SQL optimization issues with or without the skill.
The skill adds structured process (12-step workflow, strategy tracking, context coverage reporting) without degrading result quality.
All evals tested different optimization classes: sargable predicate (eval 0), function unwrap + range (eval 1), join + aggregation rewrite (eval 2).
Time and token data unavailable — live database was not running during evaluation. Rerun with active database for meaningful timing comparison.
Qualitative differences: with-skill outputs include strategy attempt logs and context coverage reports. without-skill outputs are more prose-heavy but equally correct.
Assertions focus on output correctness rather than process quality — the skill primarily adds process discipline, which is valuable for multi-step complex optimizations.
